setting.note: Roy's Kerala is a green, waterlogged world of rubber and pepper, of a pickle factory and a river that turns from playground to grave. The map gathers around Ayemenem, a real village on the Meenachal near Kottayam, then reaches west to the backwaters and north to Cochin, where the family's day at the cinema sets the tragedy moving.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Arundhati Roy, first published 1997. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The God of Small Things, Arundhati Roy (1997). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The wet green of the backwaters and the Meenachal that runs through the book. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
